opp.next_step: 3/10/26 RH Demo with Damiano Grillo. ANA stood out as key differentiator. Intake is less important as tool will be used by sourcing team. 3/06/26 RH Submitted RFI. Offered to provide a value engineering study to promote the business case.
opp.ais_why:
• Damiano (Category Manager) asked about autonomous negotiation agent capabilities and expressed interest in advanced negotiation tools, mapping to Merlin ANA (2026-02-03).
• Showed curiosity about AI-generated questions for RFPs and autonomous sourcing for tailspin, indicating alignment with Merlin AI platform potential (2026-03-11).
• Recent signals from last 3 months across 2 calls, but tempered by acknowledgment that organization is "far away" from autonomous capabilities today.

sources: Avoma 03 Feb 2026 discovery call, Damiano Grillo
Avoma 11 Mar 2026 demo call, Damiano Grillo
metrics.narrative: New Balance articulated qualitative pain but never quantified business impact. On the 03 Feb discovery call, Damiano Grillo (indirect category manager) stated that the Ariba sourcing module causes friction both internally (new hires struggle with ease of use, limited provider support) and externally (supplier complaints), and that 'a lot of the work' happens outside the tool after RFP distribution, requiring manual offline negotiation. He noted the sourcing team is growing globally (EMEA and APAC expansion) and onboarding speed is a concern. He also expressed interest in autonomous negotiation capabilities, referencing prior experience with Jaggaer's expressive feedback driving 'a lot of value.' However, no quantified metrics were provided: no cycle-time reduction target, no cost-per-event baseline, no savings-per-negotiation figure, no headcount-to-event ratio. On the 11 Mar demo, he reiterated ease-of-use and scalability as the biggest strain but again provided no numbers. The pain is real and confirmed across two calls, but the business case remains qualitative. Quantifying the value case (savings uplift, cycle-time compression, FTE efficiency gain) is the immediate next step to build executive sponsorship and budget justification.

sources: Avoma 03 Feb 2026 call, Damiano Grillo (initiated, articulated pain, committed to RFI)
Avoma 11 Mar 2026 demo call, Damiano Grillo (engaged, asked detailed questions)
107 days of silence since 11 Mar (no follow-up, no RFI, no re-engagement)
Zero other New Balance stakeholders engaged (single-threaded)
champion.narrative: Damiano Grillo (Indirect Category Manager) is the sole engaged stakeholder and is the developing champion. He initiated the relationship (reached out to Zycus via the Plan event), articulated clear pain across two calls, drove the informal demo on 11 Mar, and expressed intent to issue a formal RFI. His engagement on the 03 Feb and 11 Mar calls was substantive: he asked detailed questions about autonomous negotiation, AI-generated RFP content, Excel upload for bid sheets, and Merlin intake capabilities, and he shared internal process context (relationship-driven intake, category-management immaturity, Ariba P2P commitment, Legal owns contracts). However, his advocacy is limited: he has not introduced any other New Balance stakeholders (no SVP, no CFO, no IT, no Legal), he has not shared internal docs or a draft RFI, and he has not pushed back on any competitor or driven urgency. Most critically, he has been silent for 107 days since the 11 Mar demo. On 03 Feb he stated the RFI would be issued 'sometime in February,' but it never came. His last recorded action is requesting the demo and promising follow-up, but no follow-up occurred. This champion is cooling or has lost internal traction. He may lack the authority or executive sponsorship to move the evaluation forward without a quantified business case. His access to the Economic Buyer and Decision Maker is unknown (he has not mentioned them). Champion strength: weak to at-risk. The immediate action is to re-engage Damiano, diagnose the silence (internal blocker? budget freeze? competing priority? lost sponsorship?), co-author the business case with him, and secure an executive sponsor (SVP Strategic Sourcing) to unblock the RFI and restart momentum.

sources: Avoma 03 Feb 2026 call, Damiano Grillo (Ariba incumbent, no commitment to stay, evaluating alternatives)
Avoma 11 Mar 2026 demo call, Damiano Grillo (Ariba pain points, ANA differentiation)
Next_Step__c 02 Jun (ANA stood out as key differentiator)
No other competitor named in any source
competition.narrative: SAP Ariba is the incumbent and the sole known competitor. New Balance uses Ariba today for P2P (committed, global rollout continuing) and for the eRFX sourcing module and spend analytics (both under evaluation and at risk of replacement). On the 03 Feb call, Damiano Grillo stated that New Balance is 'early days evaluating what is out there' for the sourcing module and that there has been 'no commitment' to stay with Ariba for eRFX. He described Ariba's pain points (ease of use, supplier friction, lack of advanced negotiation, limited support, inflexible schema for spend analytics) and noted prior positive experience with Jaggaer's autonomous sourcing optimizer in a previous role, establishing Jaggaer as a known alternative reference but not a current active competitor in this cycle. No other vendors (Coupa, GEP, Ivalua, Jaggaer, Zip, Keelvar) were named across any call, Next_Step, Task, or field. Ariba's position: incumbent with embedded P2P footprint (switching cost and integration lock-in) but acknowledged weak fit for sourcing and analytics. Threat level: high, because inertia and the P2P commitment create a bias toward keeping Ariba for the full suite unless Zycus proves decisive differentiation. The wedge: Zycus Merlin AI (agentic negotiation, AI-generated RFP content, autonomous sourcing for tail spend) and ease of use (Damiano noted on 11 Mar that 'ANA stood out as key differentiator'). Activating the wedge requires a live demo of Merlin negotiation agent in action, a quantified ROI comparison (Zycus negotiation savings vs Ariba manual baseline), and reference customers in similar industries (apparel, consumer goods, global indirect procurement) who replaced Ariba sourcing with Zycus and captured measurable value.

sources: Avoma 03 Feb 2026 call, Damiano Grillo (ease of use, supplier friction, manual negotiation, team growth)
Avoma 11 Mar 2026 demo call, Damiano Grillo (scalability strain, schema inflexibility)
identify_pain.narrative: Pain is clearly identified and confirmed by Damiano Grillo, the pain owner, across two calls. The operational pain: (1) Ariba's eRFX module is difficult for internal users (new sourcing hires struggle to gain proficiency, and Ariba support is limited), (2) suppliers complain about friction when participating in New Balance sourcing events, and (3) negotiation is almost entirely manual and offline because the tool lacks advanced capabilities (Damiano stated 'we're just utilizing Ariba to send out an RFP and collect information, then we're doing a lot of the work outside the tool'). The organizational pain: the indirect sourcing team is expanding globally (EMEA and APAC regional teams added) and onboarding speed is a bottleneck. Damiano also noted on the 11 Mar call that New Balance's unique spend-category schema creates challenges with Ariba's inflexible taxonomy, limiting the value of their spend analytics. The strategic pain: without formal intake processes or advanced negotiation tools, sourcing remains relationship-driven and reactive rather than strategic, and the team cannot scale to meet enterprise demand. The pain is real, articulated by the right person (the category manager who lives it daily), and confirmed across two touchpoints. However, the pain has not been quantified (no cycle-time, cost-per-event, or savings-gap metrics), so the business case remains qualitative. The pain owner is Damiano; the executive pain owner (who would fund the fix) has not been identified.

sources: Avoma 03 Feb 2026 call, Damiano Grillo (contract management with Legal, low visibility)

paper_process.narrative: Paper process is entirely unknown. No discussion of MSA, SOW, Order Form, legal review, InfoSec questionnaire, or procurement channel has occurred across any call or field. Contract management is owned by Legal (per 03 Feb call) with 'scarce visibility' to Sourcing, but no Legal stakeholder has been introduced or engaged. New Balance runs Microsoft Dynamics as their ERP (confirmed 03 Feb call); integration requirements, data flows, and IT approval process for a new sourcing platform have not been discussed. No InfoSec review, no IT architecture deep-dive, no implementation scoping with an SI or partner. With the deal dark for 107 days and still in Formal Evaluation, these contracting and technical gates remain far out. However, the paper path will be a months-long workstream once the vendor is selected, so surfacing it early (Legal contact, InfoSec lead, IT integration owner) is a best practice. The immediate action is to ask Damiano who owns Legal/InfoSec/IT approval and to request an intro as part of re-engagement.

economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer has been identified or engaged across any call, contact role, or Salesforce field. Damiano Grillo is an indirect category manager (operational influencer), not a budget authority. On the 03 Feb call he stated that leadership would evaluate the long-term approach and that budget approval would hinge on a formal business case, but he did not name who holds budget authority. The prefetched account-level contacts include a President & CFO (Aaron Goldsmith), a VP of Transformation (Kira Zaiger Pearlman), and a SVP Strategic Sourcing & Quality (Duncan Scott, the likely functional sponsor), but none have been engaged in this cycle. With 107 days of silence and no executive conversation, the EB gap is a primary blocker. The deal cannot progress to the relevant stakeholder or Negotiation without commercial and pricing engagement at the CFO or SVP level. Mapping and engaging the Economic Buyer (likely the CFO or the SVP Strategic Sourcing given the $250k deal size and strategic procurement scope) is the rank-1 action to restart this deal.

sources: Avoma 03 Feb 2026 call, Damiano Grillo (RFI timeline, business case requirement)

decision_process.narrative: The buyer's process was stated in outline but never executed. On the 03 Feb call, Damiano Grillo described the intended path: (1) informal demo workshops with each vendor (Zycus scheduled for early March), (2) formal RFI to be issued internally in February 2026 with vendor participation, (3) leadership evaluation of RFI responses to inform the long-term sourcing approach, and (4) budget approval contingent on a business case. The 11 Mar demo occurred as planned, but the RFI never followed. Damiano noted on 03 Feb that P2P remains committed to Ariba (no change), contract management lives with Legal (low visibility, long-term conversation), and the sourcing+spend-analytics modules are the evaluation scope. He also stated that the intake process is relationship-driven today with no formal policy or threshold requiring sourcing involvement, so intake tooling is a future-phase consideration, not a near-term gate. The call sequence shows Damiano as the sole New Balance participant across both calls; no leadership, no Legal, no IT, no CFO office has joined. With 107 days of silence since the demo, the process has stalled before the RFI gate. The likely blocker: without a quantified business case and executive sponsorship, Damiano cannot drive the RFI forward. Restarting the process requires re-engaging Damiano, co-authoring the business case with him, and securing a sponsor (SVP Strategic Sourcing or VP Transformation) to greenlight the RFI and budget conversation.

sources: Avoma 03 Feb 2026 call, Damiano Grillo (ease of use, negotiation capability)
Avoma 11 Mar 2026 demo call, Damiano Grillo (scalability, schema flexibility)
Next_Step__c 02 Jun (ANA differentiation noted)
decision_criteria.narrative: Decision criteria were partially surfaced but never formalized. On the 03 Feb call, Damiano stated New Balance is evaluating what is 'best in class' for the eRFX sourcing module, with three explicit criteria: (1) ease of use for internal sourcing teams (especially new hires), (2) ease of use for suppliers participating in events, and (3) advanced negotiation capabilities (he cited Jaggaer's expressive feedback and autonomous negotiation as a reference standard). On the 11 Mar demo, he reiterated ease of use and scalability as the biggest concerns and asked detailed questions about AI-generated RFP questions, Excel upload for bid sheets, and autonomous sourcing for tail spend. He also noted that New Balance's schema definitions for spend analytics create challenges with Ariba's inflexible taxonomy, signaling that flexibility and configurability may be an unstated criterion. However, no formal RFP or RFI scoring rubric has been shared, no weighted decision matrix exists, and no technical/InfoSec/integration requirements have been discussed. The planned February RFI (which never materialized) was intended to 'collect information for leadership evaluation,' but the criteria that leadership will use remain unknown. Formalizing decision criteria in a shared scorecard (technical fit, ease of use, AI/negotiation capability, cost, integration with the relevant stakeholder, support model) is essential before any vendor-selection decision can be credible.

ai_fit_signal.summary: New Balance signals AI Curious. Damiano Grillo expressed interest in autonomous negotiation and AI-generated RFP content, and ANA stood out as a differentiator on the demo. However, no AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, or AIS_Why__c is recorded in Salesforce, and no executive-level AI appetite has been tested. The sourcing team is evaluating AI as a capability within a best-in-class sourcing tool (a feature, not a strategic priority), not as a transformation driver. AI fit is present but not a forcing function.

confidence_signals.summary: Deal is cooling and at high risk of going dark permanently. No buyer engagement in 107 days, no RFI, no executive involvement, and no forward momentum since the 11 Mar demo. The champion has not re-engaged, and the forecast (Pipeline, Dec 2026 close) is indefensible.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 03 Feb 2026, entered Formal Evaluation same day. Last verified buyer activity 11 Mar 2026 (107 days ago). Time in stage: 143+ days. Close date 18 Dec 2026 (175 days out). At 107 days dark with no RFI submission, no champion re-engagement, and no follow-on discussions, this deal has gone cold. A healthy Formal Evaluation cycle moves through demos, RFI response, shortlist decision, and deeper validation in 60-90 days; this one stalled after the second demo in March and has had zero buyer-side activity since.

north_star_verdict.headline: Deal remains stalled at 107 days of complete silence since 11 March demo. Promised February RFI never delivered, no champion contact resumed, no executive engagement exists, and no concrete path forward despite 175 days to close.

best_practice_check.summary: This deal is off track and requires urgent re-engagement and executive escalation. Three high-leverage levers: (1) restart the relationship by diagnosing the 107-day silence and co-authoring a quantified business case with Damiano; (2) secure an executive sponsor (SVP Strategic Sourcing or VP Transformation) to greenlight the RFI and budget conversation; (3) activate the Merlin AI wedge with a live negotiation-agent demo and apparel-industry references to prove decisive differentiation vs Ariba.

competitive_position.summary: SAP Ariba is the incumbent and sole known competitor, entrenched via the committed P2P global rollout but acknowledged as a weak fit for sourcing and analytics. Ariba's eRFX module is under evaluation for replacement due to ease-of-use friction (internal and supplier-side), lack of advanced negotiation capabilities, limited support, and inflexible spend-category schema. No other vendors have been named across any source. Threat level: high, driven by inertia and the P2P integration lock-in, despite clear dissatisfaction. Zycus differentiation: Merlin AI agentic negotiation (Damiano explicitly interested, referenced prior Jaggaer autonomous-sourcing success), ease of use (ANA noted as standout on 11 Mar demo), and flexible schema (addresses the Ariba analytics pain). However, the competitive battle has stalled because the deal is dark: no RFI issued, no other vendors formally engaged, no decision timeline. The current threat is not another vendor but inertia and do-nothing (New Balance continues with Ariba by default because no business case or executive sponsor has emerged to fund a replacement). Winning requires restarting momentum: re-engage Damiano, co-author a quantified business case (negotiation savings, cycle-time reduction, FTE efficiency), secure an executive sponsor (SVP Strategic Sourcing), and position Merlin AI as the wedge that Ariba cannot match.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.quote: We utilize Ariba today for eRFX sourcing, and we're looking to evaluate what other platforms can do for that space and whether it can potentially solve some pain points. Ease of use—internal and supplier-side—and advanced negotiation capabilities are the gaps. We're just utilizing Ariba to send out an RFP and collect information, then we're doing a lot of the work outside the tool. (Damiano Grillo, 03 Feb 2026). Ariba's schema definitions are inflexible and create challenges for our unique spend categories. (Damiano Grillo, 11 Mar 2026).

competitors.how_we_win: Lead with Merlin AI agentic negotiation agent (autonomous, expressive feedback, tail-spend automation) as the capability Ariba cannot match. Quantify ROI vs Ariba manual baseline (savings uplift, cycle-time compression). Surface 2-3 apparel or consumer-goods references who replaced Ariba sourcing with Zycus and captured measurable value. Position ANA (AI-generated RFP content, natural-language intake) as the ease-of-use and scalability answer for New Balance's global team expansion. Demo live negotiation session and flexible schema configuration to prove the wedge.

Position the Dec 2026 close as a forcing function (if NB wants the new platform live for FY2027 planning, the RFI and vendor selection must happen by Aug 2026). Make it easier for Damiano to move forward than to stay still.

items.grounding_quote: Pass findings from today's demo to procurement leadership and report back with next steps or feedback. (Avoma note, 11 Mar 2026). Damiano will be in touch with any next steps or feedback. (Avoma note, 11 Mar 2026)

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI positioning is strong on paper but under-activated in practice. ANA (Merlin AI-generated RFP questions and natural-language intake) was noted as a key differentiator on the 11 Mar demo. Damiano expressed explicit interest in autonomous negotiation capabilities and referenced prior success with Jaggaer's AI-driven sourcing optimizer. However, the AI narrative has not been converted into a quantified business case, a live working-session demo, or an executive-level conversation. The Merlin wedge is the path to displacing Ariba, but it must be activated with proof (live negotiation demo, reference ROI, tail-spend savings case study) to restart the deal.

items.quote: We have been growing the department, and with that comes newer associates. Is there a better tool that is ease of use internally as we continue to grow? (Damiano Grillo, 11 Mar 2026)
items.criterion: Ease of use for internal sourcing teams (especially new hires during global expansion)

items.quote: The challenges suppliers have utilizing our sourcing tool today becomes a bit of a pain point and causes friction with our supplier base. (Damiano Grillo, 03 Feb 2026)
items.criterion: Ease of use for suppliers participating in sourcing events

items.quote: Is there any capabilities that have a tool that does some more of that advanced negotiation? I know from my experience with Jaggaer's expressive feedback, those capabilities were super impactful and drove a lot of value. (Damiano Grillo, 03 Feb 2026). ANA stood out as key differentiator. (Next_Step 02 Jun, referencing 11 Mar demo)
items.criterion: Advanced negotiation capabilities (autonomous agent, expressive feedback, reverse auctions)

items.quote: New Balance's unique definitions of schema create significant challenges for their spend analytics, particularly in reconciling with the inflexible definitions set by Ariba. (Damiano Grillo, 11 Mar 2026)
items.criterion: Flexible spend-category schema (vs Ariba's rigid taxonomy)

items.quote: You guys globally rolled out Microsoft Dynamics on the ERP side. (Richard Hunsinger confirmed, 03 Feb 2026). No integration requirements or technical architecture discussion has occurred.
items.source: Avoma 03 Feb 2026 call; no follow-up technical validation

customer_expectations_fit.summary: New Balance's stated evaluation criteria—ease of use (internal and supplier), advanced negotiation capabilities, scalability for global team growth, and flexible spend-analytics schema—align well with Zycus iSource and Merlin AI strengths. ANA (AI-generated RFP questions, natural-language intake) was noted as a key differentiator on the 11 Mar demo. However, fit has not been tested against a formal RFP scorecard, no technical/integration requirements have been discussed, and no InfoSec or Legal review has occurred. Partial alignment confirmed; deeper validation (technical fit, ERP integration with Dynamics, Legal/InfoSec approval path) is required before Vendor Selected.

hard.ais_why:
• Damiano (Category Manager) asked about autonomous negotiation agent capabilities and expressed interest in advanced negotiation tools, mapping to Merlin ANA (2026-02-03).
• Showed curiosity about AI-generated questions for RFPs and autonomous sourcing for tailspin, indicating alignment with Merlin AI platform potential (2026-03-11).
• Recent signals from last 3 months across 2 calls, but tempered by acknowledgment that organization is "far away" from autonomous capabilities today.

3/10/26 RH Demo with Damiano Grillo. ANA stood out as key differentiator. Intake is less important as tool will be used by sourcing team. 

3/06/26 RH Submitted RFI. Offered to provide a value engineering study to promote the business case.

packets.subject: Richard Hunsinger

value.name: SAP Ariba
value.quote: We utilize Ariba today for eRFX sourcing, and we're looking to evaluate what other platforms can do for that space and whether it can potentially solve some pain points. Ease of use—internal and supplier-side—and advanced negotiation capabilities are the gaps. We're just utilizing Ariba to send out an RFP and collect information, then we're doing a lot of the work outside the tool. (Damiano Grillo, 03 Feb 2026). Ariba's schema definitions are inflexible and create challenges for our unique spend categories. (Damiano Grillo, 11 Mar 2026).
value.source: Avoma 03 Feb and 11 Mar 2026 calls, Damiano Grillo
value.how_we_win: Lead with Merlin AI agentic negotiation agent (autonomous, expressive feedback, tail-spend automation) as the capability Ariba cannot match. Quantify ROI vs Ariba manual baseline (savings uplift, cycle-time compression). Surface 2-3 apparel or consumer-goods references who replaced Ariba sourcing with Zycus and captured measurable value. Position ANA (AI-generated RFP content, natural-language intake) as the ease-of-use and scalability answer for New Balance's global team expansion. Demo live negotiation session and flexible schema configuration to prove the wedge.

packets.subject: SAP Ariba

value.name: Do Nothing
value.quote: 107 days of buyer silence since the 11 Mar demo. Promised RFI in February never issued. No follow-up, no executive engagement, no forward movement.
value.source: Salesforce LastActivityDate, Avoma call manifest, Next_Step__c
value.how_we_win: Re-engage Damiano with a forcing event: co-author the business case (quantified pain: manual negotiation cost, onboarding delay, supplier-friction impact), offer a value-engineering study (referenced in Next_Step 06 Mar but never delivered), and secure an intro to the SVP Strategic Sourcing (executive sponsor to greenlight RFI and budget). Position the Dec 2026 close as a forcing function (if NB wants the new platform live for FY2027 planning, the RFI and vendor selection must happen by Aug 2026). Make it easier for Damiano to move forward than to stay still.

packets.subject: Do Nothing

gaps: Only 1 buyer-side attendee (Damiano Grillo) across all calls; no other New Balance stakeholder engaged (no SVP, CFO, IT, Legal, Finance)
No AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, or AIS_Why__c in Salesforce; AI appetite inferred from calls only
No OpportunityLineItem (product line-item pricing) records; $250k Amount is placeholder, no quote breakdown
Zero OpportunityContactRole records; stakeholder map built from call attendees and account-level contacts only
No field-history movement on Stage, Amount, CloseDate, or Forecast since creation; deal mechanics frozen since 03 Feb
No completed Tasks or Events recorded post-demo (11 Mar onward); activity-tracking gap
One call (04 Mar demo workshop) discovered but not recorded (gap in call content, touchpoint counted)
validation gate: a named person in a to-do/action item is in no Salesforce contact role, Avoma attendee list, or active-user roster and is anchored to no source (offending: 'SVP Strategic Sourcing')
